observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed
through the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to: (a)
adopt appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash
advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; (b) file a request
for authority to write off all dormant unliquidated cash advances pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2023-008; (c) discontinue the granting of cash advances
that exceed the maximum accountability limits and if deemed necessary,
increase the fidelity bond premiums of the accountable officers; (d) submit the
lacking documents as enumerated above and ensure that the appropriate
documentary requirements are attached to the liquidation reports as required
by COA Circular 2012-001; and (e) continue to strictly observe the provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of
cash advances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) Accounting
and Treasury Offices to: (i) reconcile their records on RPT/SET receivables to
ensure accurate and reliable reporting; and (b) Acting Municipal Treasurer
to: (i) employ effective collection strategies by updating and posting the
notices of delinquency for property taxes at designated areas and conducting
massive campaigns and public information dissemination on the value of
paying taxes; and (ii) if warranted, enforce the provision of Section 254 (b)
of the RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) Municipal
Accountant and the GSO to: (i) complete and reconcile their respective PPE
records; (ii) exert more efforts in locating documents pertinent to the
acquisition of its PPEs; (iii) maintain their respective PPE records; and (iv)
thereafter, conduct at least on a quarterly basis, the reconciliation of their
PPE information; and (b) Municipal Accountant to recognize impairment
losses in the books of accounts for all identified unserviceable assets in
accordance with applicable accounting standards; (c) Disposal Committee to
facilitate disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with the prescribed
guidelines and procedures; and (d) GSO to ensure that all operational and
roadworthy government vehicles are duly registered with the LTO and
insured with the GSIS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance
of the Due to NGAs account; and (b) Acting Municipal Treasurer to: (i)
reconcile the Due to LGUs account of the Municipality with the Provincial
Government’s records; and (ii) if warranted, return the unremitted amount to
the Provincial Government. Furthermore, we recommended that Management
avail of the one-time cleansing of all dormant accounts as provided by COA
Circular No. 2023-008.

WE recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) MHRMO to
continue updating and reconciling leave credits of employees of the
Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and
MHRMO to develop a structured plan to integrate the recognition of Leave
Benefits Payable within the three-year transition period as prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2024-009.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) BPLO to
strictly enforce compliance with tax assessment requirements by verifying
gross sales declarations against actual financial records and ITR submissions;
and (b) MTO to: (i) proactively conduct examinations of business books of
accounts as mandated by law and the Municipal Revenue Code; and (ii)
implement immediate reassessment of businesses that have under-declared
their gross receipts to recover lost revenues.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) HRMO to: (i)
revise and improve the existing hiring process for JO personnel by clearly
delineating the responsibilities between the HRMO and the Office of the
Mayor; and (ii) require the aforesaid JOs who failed to physically report to
personally appear before the AT and present their municipal-issued
identification cards for verification; (b) Municipal Accountant: (i) ensure
completeness of supporting documents to substantiate all payroll claims; and
(ii) immediately submit the lacking documents, in proper form, to the Office of
the Auditor to facilitate appropriate audit action; and (c) Acting Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that wages are paid directly to the claimants/payees,
otherwise, require the submission of a written authorization along with valid
identification cards of both the payee and the representative, and attach the
same to the paid payrolls.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MSWDO to
formulate policy on the grant of AICS that would define or designate, among
others the (i) procedures to be employed on proper assessment; (ii)
commensurate amount of grants for designated condition and/or situation;
and (iii) qualifications/eligibilities of the beneficiaries.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the LSB, Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant to: (a) promptly formulate a comprehensive
three-year program in accordance with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1,
series of 2017; (b) ensure that the three-year program aligns with the priorities and
objectives outlined in the circular, particularly in financing priority PPAs aimed at
supporting the education sector; (c) implement measures to enhance planning
mechanisms to ensure better allocation and management of SEF resources, with
primary focus on addressing the educational needs of students in Cuyapo; (d)
discontinue the allocation of funds for expenses that are not among those
prioritized to be charged against the SEF; (e) justify and/or submit legal basis on
the aforesaid non-priority expenses; and (f) ensure proper compliance with posting
requirements as required by the relevant Procurement Law.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MPDO and
Municipal Engineer to: (a) strengthen its planning and monitoring
mechanisms to ensure timely project execution; and (b) ensure that the
Municipality’s Comprehensive Development Plan will be updated consistent
with item 3.2.1 of the aforesaid Circular.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MPDO, MBO, and
LDRRMO to: (a) strengthen planning and monitoring to optimize the use of
the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) in line
with the LDRRM Plans by: (i) improving coordination with various
organizations to maximize seminar funding opportunities; (ii) discontinuing
the allocation of standby funds for equipment and instead identifying specific
items to purchase with the 70% Mitigation Fund; (iii) immediately request
turnover documents for the evacuation center and apply for GSIS insurance;
and (iv) ensure proper planning of all LDRRMF projects and coordination
with NGAs and other Government agencies; and (b) ensure that the 6-Year
DRRMP will be updated consistent with Section 12 paragraph 6 of the
Republic Act No. 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Act of 2010.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the GFPS to: (a)
establish and maintain an independent, regularly updated GAD database
containing sex-disaggregated data; (b) ensure periodic data updates through
coordination with barangays and relevant agencies; (c) adopt the parameters
of appropriate cost distribution and gender analysis for its PPAs using the
HGDG tools to ensure effective resource allocation and program
implementation; and (d) establish stricter monitoring and documentation
processes to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and improve the
effectiveness of gender-responsive initiatives.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Treasurer to continue the practice of complying with the withholding and
remittance of taxes due the BIR in accordance with Section 114 (c) of the
National Revenue Code as amended by RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation
No. 2-98.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to ensure performance of its
mandate as withholding agent, with emphasis on strict observance of
regulation on timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employer’s
contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated
May 30, 1997; and (b) continue to coordinate with GSIS on the reconciliation
and disposition of the outstanding balance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to: (a) observe the practice of
remitting Pag-IBIG premiums on a timely basis so that the employees can
enjoy all the privileges of a Pag-IBIG member; and (b) ensure that the balance
of Due to Pag-IBIG account is accurately presented, addressing any
outstanding arrears as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Accounting and
Treasurer’s Office to: (a) continue the practice of intact and timely
remittances of employees’ and employer’s contributions due to PhilHealth in
accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of
2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014; (b) expedite completion of needed
documents for the pending reconciliation on the unremitted balances as per
PhilHealth Record; and (c) ensure that the arrears from the previous MOA
will be funded and settled.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continually ensure
observance of applicable rules and regulations in the receipt and use of
government funds and property.

We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor:
(a) diligently pursue all possible actions to ensure the full settlement or
liquidation of cash advances that are due and demandable, including the
enforcement of legal remedies such as withholding salaries and filing of
appropriate sanctions against the personnel concerned; and
(b) as warranted, consider the filing before the Commission on Audit of
request for write-off of cash advances that met the requirements of COA
Circular 2016-005.

observations.recommendation:
1.14 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to
(a) diligently pursue all possible actions to ensure the full settlement
or liquidation of cash advances that are due and demandable,
including the enforcement of legal remedies such as withholding
salaries and filing of appropriate sanctions against the personnel
concerned;
(b) as warranted, consider the filing before the Commission on Audit
of request for write-off of cash advances that met the requirements of COA Circular 2016-005.

2.26 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) GSO to
prioritize the preparation and/or reconstruction of property records
and registries; and (b) Municipal Inventory Committee to (i) attach
property tags and property numbers to each PPE item adopting the
prescribed numbering system of COA Circular No. 2020-006; (ii)
assign value to the PPE found at station through application of the
options presented in the same Circular; and (iii) perform the
mandated procedures for the disposal of the identified unserviceable
properties.

4.21 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MPDO to-
a. align the AIP with the CLUP, CDP, and LDIP to ensure
realization of the Municipality’s development goals; and
b. see to it that the development programs are well-planned and
procurement –and –implementation ready.

5.23 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the LDRRMO in
collaboration with the LDRRMC to -
(a) formulate a medium-term LDRRMP which shall give direction
to the annual disaster risk reduction and management activities;
(b) carefully plan for and monitor the utilization of LDRRMF to
maximize its purpose; and
(c) propose for the provision of suitable storage space intended solely
for the stockpiling requirements of disaster response.

6.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MENRO to-
a. regularly assess, monitor, and update, as warranted, the SWMP
projections on waste and revenue generation;
b. encourage stakeholders to actively participate in minimizing
waste generation through regular IEC activities;
c. provide enough storage area in the MRF for the by-products of
their waste diversion activities; and
d. have a concrete plan for the sale or disposition of the products
of their waste diversion activities.

7.19 We recommended and the LCE agreed to-
(a) direct the BPLO to -
i. ensure strict enforcement of the submission of ITRs and
related documents, as applicable;
ii. revisit the assessment made on the identified taxpayers,
and collect the outstanding business tax, as warranted;
iii. henceforth, see to it that all establishments doing business
in the Municipality have valid business permits; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to –
i. conduct regular examination of books of accounts of
businesses; and
ii. ensure diligent collection of business taxes from all eligible
entities.

8.23 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GAD FP to-
(a) carefully plan the corresponding budget allocations of GAD PPAs
by preparing project breakdown of expenses before the approval
of the plan and budget; and
(b) create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis for the annual GAD
Plans and Budget by using the templates provided in PCW
Memorandum Circular No. 2018-04 dated September 19, 2018;
(c) conduct a review after three years and an end-term evaluation to
reflect on the status of accomplishment, variances, and overall
assessment of the implementation of the GAD agenda based on the
goals and desired results/outcomes, and report thereon using the
prescribed format.
(d) use the HGDG tool to properly identify the gender issues on LGU
PPAs or its portion that can be attributed to GAD Activities

9.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to continue its
compliance with the Bureau’s relevant regulations on the
withholding of the correct amount of taxes due and the remittances
thereof within the prescribed period.

10.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to coordinate
with the GSIS and seek for their assistance to properly address the
differences in the accounts’ balances.

11.17 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Accounting
and Treasurer’s Office to –
a. expedite completion of needed documents for the pending
reconciliation on the unremitted balances as per PhilHealth
Record;
b. review the MOA entered by the Municipality with PhilHealth in
CYs 2005 and 2013 on the Medicare for indigents, and issue
payment, if warranted.

12.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to continue
their compliance with their obligation to collect and to remit the
Contributions and Loan Repayments in accordance with the
provisions of R.A. No. 9679.

13.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to once and for all require
the persons responsible for the immediate settlement of the audit
suspensions and, in the event of failure of said persons responsible,
to take appropriate action way forward, in accordance with
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant to
refrain from processing
additional cash advances
unless the previous one is
liquidated; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to
(i) monitor the drawing of
cash advances of the
disbursing officer to ensure
observance of the maximum
amounts allowed based on
the fidelity bond coverage;
(ii) stop the practice of
transferring cash advances to
other AOs; and
(iii) recommend for the
designation of additional
disbursing officer/s based on
the needs of the
municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) recognize liabilities only when there is verified accomplishment and once the obligation is certified payable and
(b) record withholding taxes for accruals as at year-end.

We recommended that the
LCE require the -
(a) MHO and Municipal
Accountant to reconcile data
and reports to arrive at the
remaining balance which
shall be costed and recorded
in the books and registries
that is to be consequently
expensed when the utilization
of the same is reported;
(b) MHO to prospectively
provide the Municipal
Accountant with the Property
Transfer Reports (PTRs)
acknowledging the receipt
and the resulting inventory
reports of remaining
donations in-kind related to
the COVID-19 vaccination
program, or for any other
public purpose for that
matter; and
(c) Municipal Accountant to
subsequently recognize in the
books of the Municipality the
receipts of in-kind donations
at cost or at their fair market
values whichever information
is available pursuant to
Paragraph 96 of IPSAS 23.

We recommended the Implemented
Management to continue to
comply with the guidelines
on the receipt, utilization and
accounting of fund transfers
received from National and
Government Agencies.

We recommended that the
LCE instruct the HRMO to:
(i) formulate policies to
ensure that the personnel to
be hired has the proper skills
and competence to deliver the
services required based on
specific duties;
(ii) execute individual
contracts to which the
services of the JOs and COS
shall be based upon; and
(iii) ensure the validity of
claims through requiring
complete supporting
documentation prior to the
payment.

We recommended that the
LCE require the BAC to:
(a) ensure that the winning
bidder post the required
Performance Security and
enter into a contract with the
Procuring Entity within ten
(10) calendar days from
receipt by the winning bidder
of the Notice of Award;
(b) stop the practice of
issuing Notice to Proceed
before the posting of
Performance Security;
(c) require the completeness
of the details of the
Performance Bond such as
the existence and accuracy of
the effectivity period;
(d) disqualify the bidder that
failed to post the Performance
Security and/or sign the
contract within ten (10)
calendar days from receipt
without justifiable reason and
undertake post-qualification
for the next-ranked Lowest
Calculated Bid or Highest
Rated Bid;
(e) ensure that the
procurements are
meticulously and judiciously
planned to avoid delays and
possible loss;
(f) see to it that no work shall
commence unless the
contractor has submitted the
required documentary
requirements and the
procuring entity has given
written approval; and
(g) religiously update the
Procurement Monitoring
Report (PMR) and
communicate the lapses noted
to the Project Monitoring
Committee for appropriate
actions.

We commended Management Implemented
in exerting efforts in the
withholding and remittance of
taxes from government
money payments, and
recommended, to which the
LCE agreed, to continue the
timely and full remittance of
taxes withheld in compliance
with BIR Regulations.

We recommended that the Implemented
LCE require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to once and for
all coordinate with the
PhilHealth for the
reconciliation and proper
disposition of the
₱120,493.96 unidentified
balance of Due to PhilHealth
account.

We recommended that the Implemented
LCE require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to continue the
prompt remittance of all
contributions and loan
amortizations to HDMF.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to consider the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to improve the reliability of its PPE accounts, and eventually, its financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to 

(a) coordinate with the Philhealth for the reconciliation and proper disposition of the 

₱112,693.96 unidentified balance of Due to Philhealth account.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to 

(i) continue the prompt remittance of all contributions and loan amortizations to HDMF; 

(ii) coordinate with the HDMF for the reconciliation and proper disposition of the 

₱72,650.32 unidentified balance of Due to Pag-IBIG account.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) coordinate with the GSIS for the reconciliation and disposition of the unidentified year-end balance; and 

(b) remit the balance to the GSIS in accordance with pertinent law and regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
continue planning and implementing DRRM programs and projects
that are relevant and timely.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the MPDO to
observe the reporting and monitoring required for fund transfers from
National Government; and (b) continue to comply with the guidelines
on the receipt, utilization and accounting thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) create an Inventory
Committee composed of adequate number of members at least one
member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the
agency; and (b) instruct the inventory committee to carefully plan and
conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in accordance
with the guidelines to improve the reliability of its PPE accounts, and
eventually, its financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Engineer to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of
development projects; (b) MPDO to (i) provide specific implementation
schedule for each PPA in the AIP; and (ii) continue identifying high-
impact capital expenditures projects consistent with the Medium-Term
Development Plan of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GFPS to:
a. continue to comply with the allocation of the prescribed budget for
GAD and the preparation of a relevant GAD Budget and Plan

calendar days from receipt without justifiable reason and undertake
post-qualification for the next-ranked Lowest Calculated Bid or

We recommended and management agreed that  the  Municipal  Mayor
        instruct the Municipal Accountant  to  (a)  require  the  concerned
        personnel to liquidate/submit liquidation reports as  soon  as  the
        purpose thereof had been accomplished and refund/return the  excess
        cash advance, if any, for the full settlement of cash advance;  (b)
        update and monitor regularly the liquidation of cash  advances  and
        send demand letters to the personnel with outstanding cash advance;
        and (c) refrain from granting new cash advance to personnel without
        the liquidation of the previous cash advance.

We reiterated our recommendation and management agreed that  the
        Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) revert  to
        the unappropriated surplus the accounts  payable  that  were  found
        without valid claims and have been outstanding  for  two  years  or
        over; and (b) continue exerting effort to cleanse  the  account  of
        liabilities that are of doubtful validity.

We recommended and management agreed  that  the  Municipal
        Mayor  instruct   the   Municipal   Accountant   to   (a)   conduct
        reconciliation of the Due from other Funds and Due to  other  Funds
        accounts; and (b) effect the necessary adjustments to  bring  their
        balances into agreement.

We  recommended  and  the  management  agreed  to  (a)  continue
        develop/invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in  the
        utilization of its 20% Development Fund and  ensure  the  effective
        and efficient implementation of the projects; and (b) instruct  the
        Municipal  Engineer  to  (i)  strictly  monitor  implementation  of
        awarded projects to ensure that these are completed  as  scheduled;
        and (ii) compute for the liquidated damages to be  imposed  to  the
        contractors  for  failure  to  complete  the  projects  within  the
        specified contract time.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor to
        (1) ensure the maximum utilization of LDRRMF  to  enhance  disaster
        preparedness and response capabilities; and (2) instruct the LDRRMO
        and BAC to ensure the  timely  procurement  and  implementation  of
        LDRRM PPAs.

We recommended and management agreed that  the  Municipal  Mayor
        thru the MENRO (a) continue to embark  on  effective  projects  and
        programs that support the  Municipality’s  Ecological  Solid  Waste
        Management Program; and (b)  if  funds  allow,  consider  extending
        financial assistance, resources, and equipment to the 22  barangays
        which have remained non-complaint in establishing a functional  MRF
        as required by law.

We recommended and management  agreed  to  observe  the  prudent
        utilization of the SEF  and  discontinue  appropriating  funds  for
        expenses which are not valid charges to the fund.

We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed  that
        the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget  Officer  to  (a)
        observe strictly the general limitations on personal services cited
        under Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and Item 4.2 of LBC  No.  98  dated
        October 14, 2011; and  (b)  ensure  that  the  Municipality  has  a
        balanced budget  and  that  enough  funds  are  allocated  for  the
        delivery of basic and development projects.

We reiterated our recommendation and management agreed that the
        Municipal Mayor require:


        a. the Municipal Treasurer or  the  Disbursing  Officer  to  effect
              payment of goods and  services  direct  to  the  supplier  or
              dealer except in  cases  where  needed  items  are  extremely
              necessary and urgent  in  which  case  reimbursement  may  be
              allowed; and


        b. discourage the practice of procuring goods and services  through
              reimbursement basis or cash advance, unless the  item/service
              is petty in nature.

We commended the effort of  the  management  in  surpassing  the
        targeted collections for various income accounts.  Nonetheless,  we

        attainable performance targets on all possible income accounts.

   the Municipality to  ascertain  and  pinpoint  the  causes/s  of
   incurrence of  cash  deficit  and  assertively  pursue  feasible
   solutions that can address the issue.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor  agreed  to  require  the
Municipal Accountant to:


a) regularly prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation  Statements
   of all cash in bank accounts within ten (10) days  from  receipt
   of the Bank Statements;


b) investigate the causes of the discrepancies in the book and bank
   record and to effect the necessary adjustments in the  books  to
   establish the correct account balance; and

c)       substantiate the reconciling items presented in the BRS by
   coordinating with the depository bank.

We  recommended  and  the  management  agreed  to  instruct  the
Municipal Accountant to:


 (a)       require  the  concerned  personnel  to   liquidate/submit
     liquidation reports as soon as the purpose  thereof  had  been
     accomplished and refund/return the excess cash advance, if any
     for the full settlement of cash advance;


 (b)      update and monitor regularly  the  timely  liquidation  of
     cash advances and send demand letters to  the  personnel  with
     outstanding cash advance;


  (c)     refrain from accepting liquidation reports with unutilized
     portion of the cash advance not yet returned; and


 (d)      refrain  from  granting  new  cash  advance  to  personnel
     without the liquidation of the previous cash advance.

We reiterated our recommendations and the management agreed to:


a.     prioritize the initiation of comprehensive physical  count
   of its property in order to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers
   supporting the reliability of books; and


b.     maintain property  records  in  accordance  with  the  New
   Government Accounting Manual to  establish  custodianship  and
   assign accountability; and


c.      reclassify completed  projects  in  the  CIP  account  to
   appropriate PPE account;


d.     work back on  past  transactions  to  trace  prior  years’
   acquisition of PPE to be able to provide depreciation and  the
   corresponding accumulated depreciation on depreciable property
   items.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to implement
the following courses of action:


a.    initiate appropriate action for the titling of all the  land
   owned by the Municipal Government of Cuyapo and  register  them
   in its name to  establish  full  ownership,  legal  rights  and
   obligations over the property; and


b.     instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  exert  effort  to
   determine the complete and  accurate  details  to  support  the
   general  ledger  balance   of   land   account   amounting   to
   P3,233,149.11.

We recommended  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to
conduct reconciliation of the Due from other Funds and Due to other
Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of  the  discrepancy/ies  in
the balances of the  two  accounts  and  to  effect  the  necessary
adjustments to bring their balances into agreement.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:


a.  instruct  the  BAC  to  conduct  competitive  bidding  in   the
   procurement of consultancy services  or  resort  to  alternative
   mode of procurement in accordance with R.A.  No.  9184  and  its
   RIRR;


b. refrain from engaging services of persons who are not experts in
   their  field  or  do  not  have  qualifications  to  be   called
   consultants whose services are unnecessary in the operations  of
   the Municipality; and


c. immediately stop the practice  of  hiring  private  lawyers  for
   purposes that are not within the granted functions prescribed by
   Revised Guidelines on the  Deputation  of  Private  Lawyers  and
   Special Attorneys and without obtaining the required  clearances
   from the OSG prior to their engagement.

We  recommended  and  the  Municipal  Mayor  agreed  to  observe
strictly the general limitations on personal services  cited  under
Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and Item 4.2 of LBC No. 98 dated  October
14, 2011 to ensure that the  Municipality  shall  have  a  balanced
budget and that enough funds are  allocated  for  the  delivery  of
basic and development projects.


We also recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  being  the  Local
Chief  Executive  ensure  that  the  budgeting  requirements   were
completely complied with and  payments  of  personal  services  are
within the budget limitations to avoid expenditures  wanting  legal
basis.

We recommended that the management cause the following:


a)  increase the fidelity bond of the Municipal Social Welfare  and
   Development Officer to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum
   accountability that she handles for a given period; and


b) Refrain from granting cash advances to its employees not covered
   by the fidelity bond or employees whose  fidelity  bond  already
   expired.

We recommended and that the Local Chief Executive:


1)  instruct  the  Disbursing  Officer  to   (a)   make   necessary
   adjustments  for  the  unrecorded  cash  advances  amounting  to
   P89,000.00, unliquidated cash advance amounting  to  P940,000.00
   and rectify the noted errors in the cashbook and;  (b)  maintain
   the cashbook following the guidelines set forth in  Section  28,
   Volume II of Manual on the New Government Accounting System; and



2) require the Municipal Accountant and the accountable officers to
   regularly reconcile their records as stated in Item  7,  Chapter
   II of Revised Cash Examination Manual.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed  to  require
the Municipal Engineer to  comply  with  the  required  posting  of
information and publicity on  programs/projects/activities  of  the
Municipality and to inform the audit team within ten days after the
award   of   the   project   or   before   the   start    of    the
program/project/activity that the  appropriate  project  signboards
and/or public notices are already posted at the project site.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:  (a)  the
Municipality’s Bids and  Awards  Committee  (BAC)  to  prepare  the
Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) to be approved by the  head  of
the  procuring  entity  and  submit  the  same  to  the  Government
Procurement and Policy  Board  (GPPB)  in  printed  and  electronic
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after each semester;  and
(b) the BAC Secretariat to post all Notices of Awards  and  Notices
to  Proceed,  together  with  the  approved   contracts   for   the
procurement of infrastructure projects, goods, and services in  the
PhilGEPS, the agency website and in any conspicuous  place  in  the
premises of the Municipality within the prescribed period  pursuant
to the above cited provisions in the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly follow  the
guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF and refrain from  using
the 70% Mitigation Fund for purposes unrelated to the disaster risk
reduction and management.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed  to  require
the Budget  Officer,  Municipal  Engineer  and  Municipal  Planning
Development Officer to strictly utilize the  20%  Development  Fund
for eligible expenditures only.

We recommended that Municipal Local School Board (1) ensure  the
effective and efficient implementation and monitoring of  the  PPAs
indicated in the approved budget (2) coordinate with the  concerned
Municipal officials with regards to budgeting of SEF (3)  consider
the unappropriated cash balance at the beginning of  the  year  and
include  the  same  in  determining  the  amount   of   funds   for
appropriation in the annual budget for SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MENRO
to continue the close monitoring of barangays’ compliance with  the
implementation of  existing  environmental  laws  and  regulations;
maintain  coordination  with  Barangay  Officials  to  sustain  the
constituents’ obedience on “no segregation,  no  collection  policy
and the “no littering policy”; and exercise strong  political  will
to implement the five basic environmental laws  on  preserving  and
protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:


a. properly establish the GAD Focal  Point  System  with  Technical
   Working Group and Executive Committee;


b. send all members of the GFPS including elected  local  officials
   and department heads  to  capacity  building  programs  and  GAD
   seminars;


c. encourage all members of the  GFPS  to  function  and  discharge
   their duties accordingly; and


d. instruct the members of the GFPS to  (i)  prepare  proposal  for
   PPAs earlier to give proper time for their review and  approval;
   and (ii) analyze each activities for appropriate  allocation  of
   fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (LCE)  and  other
concerned officials  to  exert  more  effort  to  obtain  ownership
records/documents  on  the  all   insurable   properties   of   the
municipality; complete database  of  information  thereon;  and  to
secure adequate insurance  for  all  insurable  properties  of  the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  to  instruct  the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:


a. adhere strictly to the provisions  of  RA  No.  8291  on  prompt
   remittance of amount due to GSIS to avoid imposition of  penalty
   and interest on delayed or non-remittance thereof; and


b. exert effort to determine/identify the errors  and  deficiencies
   committed  in  recording  of  prior   years’   withholding   and
   remittance of GSIS premium and adjust the same, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a)  instruct  the
Municipal Accountant and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  cause  the
timely and full remittance of the total taxes withheld every month,
in conformity with BIR Regulations, thereby  helping  the  national
government in raising additional revenues; and  (b)  the  Municipal
Accountant to exert more effort to determine the transactions where
unremitted transactions arise.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to enforce and require the
immediate settlement of suspensions and disallowances by the liable
and responsible persons.
